items.headline: Auto Sales Use Approved for W. El Camino Real Commercial Site
items.summary: The Planning Commission approved a special development permit allowing auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real. The project involves site modifications including repaving, restriping, and landscaping upgrades, with no building expansion or exterior changes required.

items.headline: 195-unit apartment complex permitted near Berryessa transit station
items.summary: A $32 million multi-family development received permits at 825 Berryessa Station Way, adding significant housing capacity near the Berryessa BART station. The project aligns with San José's transit-oriented development goals in an area primed for density.

items.headline: Water bill assistance program launches for struggling customers
items.summary: The City Council approved a Financial Assistance Pilot Program to help water customers pay their bills. The program aims to support residents facing water service affordability challenges.

items.headline: City in potential litigation over Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a closed session on May 12 to discuss potential litigation arising from a water main incident at Cuesta Park. The legal discussion also involved real property negotiations for parcels at 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue.

items.headline: Planning Commission to review 27-unit townhome project with affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission is scheduled to consider a residential development featuring 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. The project represents a mixed-income housing addition to Cupertino's residential stock.

items.headline: Litigation involving SummerHill Homes and city continues
items.summary: A lawsuit, Mark Fantozzi v. City of Cupertino (with SummerHill Homes as a real party in interest), remains in active litigation. The council discussed the case in closed session on May 11.

items.headline: City Council to hear 72-unit affordable housing project on Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The Council will hold a public hearing on a six-story, 100% affordable housing development proposed for 450 Lytton Avenue, which would replace a city-owned surface parking lot with 72 residential units on a 0.43-acre parcel. The developer is requesting density bonus concessions and waivers under state law to enable the project.

items.headline: Council adopts policy restricting city property use for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: Campbell City Council adopted a resolution prohibiting the use of city-owned and city-operated property for federal civil enforcement actions. This policy limits how federal agencies can access municipal facilities and land for immigration, tax, or other civil enforcement activities.
